nodes.label: Announcement
nodes.expandedContent: The Election Commission of India holds a press conference to announce the full schedule of the elections, including all polling phases and counting dates.

nodes.label: MCC Begins
nodes.description: The Model Code of Conduct comes into force.
nodes.expandedContent: Starts the same day as election announcement. Ends after counting is complete. Government cannot announce new welfare schemes or use government vehicles for campaigns.

nodes.label: Nominations
nodes.expandedContent: Candidates submit their nomination forms along with affidavits detailing their assets, liabilities, and any criminal records to the Returning Officer. Deadlines vary per phase.

nodes.label: Scrutiny
nodes.expandedContent: The Returning Officer checks all submitted nomination papers to ensure they meet legal requirements. Invalid nominations are rejected.

nodes.label: Withdrawal
nodes.expandedContent: Candidates are given a few days after scrutiny to formally withdraw their candidacy if they choose not to contest.

nodes.label: Voting Phases
nodes.expandedContent: Voters cast their votes using EVMs at designated polling booths. The staggered phases allow for the movement of security forces across the country.

nodes.label: Counting
nodes.description: Votes are counted and results declared.
nodes.expandedContent: EVMs are brought to counting centers. The votes are tallied under the supervision of the Returning Officer and candidate agents. Results are declared constituency by constituency.

nodes.label: Swearing In
nodes.expandedContent: The party or coalition with a majority in the Lok Sabha stakes a claim to form the government, and the President administers the oath of office to the Prime Minister and Council of Ministers.
